gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: ETH build interval accumulation cap enables full-pool incentive drain via swapETHForTorusAndBuild
candidate.claimed_root_cause: When many intervals are missed, _calculateIntervalsETHBuild caps _totalAmountForInterval at totalETHForBuild, causing the 1.5% incentive to apply to the entire pool rather than a single interval allocation
candidate.claimed_impact: Unprivileged attacker receives 1.5% of entire totalETHForBuild pool in one call after 30+ days of inactivity

live_preconditions.name: timeElapsed since lastETHBuildIntervalStartTimestamp >= 30 days
live_preconditions.observed_value: ~10709 seconds (~18 minutes) elapsed at capture time 2026-07-14T09:58:29Z vs lastETHBuildIntervalStartTimestamp=1784012400

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Computes amountPerInterval * (missedIntervals+1) and caps at totalETHForBuild; after 30+ days the cap triggers, setting _totalAmountForInterval = totalETHForBuild

IntervalAlreadyUsed guard only checks amountBurned != 0; incentive = currInterval.amountAllocated * 150 / 10000 sent to msg.sender; if amountAllocated == totalETHForBuild, attacker receives 1.5% of entire pool
INCENTIVE_FEE_BUILD=150, BPS_DENOM=10000, INTERVALS_PER_DAY=144, INTERVAL_TIME=10 minutes confirm arithmetic in the report

call_sequence: Wait until block.timestamp - lastETHBuildIntervalStartTimestamp >= 30 days (currently ~30 days away from live state)

extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: totalETHForBuild * 150 / 10000 = ~1,599,520,501,288,770 wei (~0.0016 ETH at current pool size; scales with pool growth)
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol loses 1.5% of entire ETH build pool in one call instead of 1.5% of a single interval allocation (~0.069% of pool)
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Design intent is per-interval incentive of amountPerInterval * 150 / 10000 (~0.001% of pool per call). Cap collapses all missed intervals into one allocation equal to totalETHForBuild, making the incentive 1.5% of the entire pool. Attacker is entitled to incentive on one interval's worth of ETH, not the entire pool balance.

setup: Record attacker EOA with no special privileges
vm.warp(block.timestamp + 31 days) to satisfy 30-day gap precondition
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If attacker receives only a single interval's incentive (~totalETHForBuild / 1440 * 150 / 10000) rather than 1.5% of the full pool, the cap does not trigger as described and the finding does not hold

rejection_reason: Not rejected. Downgraded from HIGH_CONFIDENCE_CANDIDATE to NEEDS_LOCAL_PROOF because: (1) the critical precondition of 30+ days elapsed since lastETHBuildIntervalStartTimestamp is NOT currently met — live state shows only ~18 minutes elapsed at capture time, so the exploit requires waiting ~30 days from the capture block; (2) current extractable value is small (~0.0016 ETH) though it scales with pool deposits; (3) a pinned-fork test with vm.warp is required to confirm the cap triggers and the incentive equals 1.5% of the full pool rather than a single interval. The code path and arithmetic are confirmed correct in source; the finding is plausible and warrants local proof.
